authoritative_sources.title: 15 CFR Part 30, Foreign Trade Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. export-reporting regulation for EEI filing obligations, filing timing, exemptions, corrections, and record responsibilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Export intake, filing readiness, and completion validation

authoritative_sources.title: AESDirect User Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Census system guide for AES filing workflow, ITN issuance, proof-of-filing citations, and message handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 15 CFR Part 758, Export Clearance Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of Industry and Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary BIS export-clearance reference for when EEI, licensing references, and carrier documentation affect shipment release and export progression.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Shipment release and export-clearance decision points

authoritative_sources.title: Freight Forwarder Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of Industry and Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official BIS workflow guidance on SLI, written authorization, forwarder-exporter responsibilities, and export red flags in operational practice.

authoritative_sources.title: Schedule B Search Engine
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Census export commodity classification source used to normalize Schedule B terminology and support evidence quality in EEI filings.

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance on End-User and End-Use Controls and U.S. Person Controls
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, Bureau of Industry and Security
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official BIS control-boundary guidance for end-user, end-use, and red-flag conditions that can stop or reroute export execution.

authoritative_sources.title: Sanctions Programs and Country Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary sanctions authority baseline for country and party restrictions that can block export execution or require compliance escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory on Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Treasury AML guidance for suspicious trade patterns that can change whether an export workflow may safely proceed.
used_for: suspicious invoice or shipment pattern handling

authoritative_sources.title: Trade-Based Money Laundering: Risk Indicators
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international risk-control framework for TBML indicators across business structure, trade activity, documents, commodities, and account activity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk-control design for suspicious trade activity

authoritative_sources.title: WCO Data Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized customs data standard for harmonized data elements and consistent field-level evidence handling across export systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Artifact and field normalization across systems

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate USPPI, consignee, routed or non-routed status, filing authority, commercial documents, and jurisdictional prerequisites before any filing recommendation.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Export packet assembly and data normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize commodity, destination, quantity, value, Schedule B, and license-reference data across documents and systems before submission or update.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: EEI filing, response handling, and state progression
workflow_stages.description: Move work through ready-to-file, filed, verify, fatal-error, hold, rework, departed, and completed states based on AES or broker-system evidence and authoritative rules.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Document release, departure, and closeout handling
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate ITN or exemption citation placement, carrier-document release, departure confirmation, and post-filing corrections without skipping required evidence.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, sanctions, and end-use escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious trade patterns, sanctions risks, or end-use concerns introduce regulated-risk or financial-impact conditions outside the lane.

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce disposition and completion records only when filing outcome, carrier-document release, departure or approved closure, and required evidence references are present.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: Commercial invoice, packing list, booking confirmation, bill of lading or airway bill, and shipper's letter of instruction
USPPI, consignee, forwarder, routed-export, and written filing-authority records
Schedule B references, ECCN or EAR99 declarations, license or license-exception references, and destination data
AES EEI filings, ITN or exemption citations, verify messages, fatal-error details, and correction history
Carrier document-release references and departure confirmation artifacts
Commercial value, payment-impact, reconciliation, and communication-log artifacts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, approval-history, and escalation records
systems_of_record: Export operations systems and case queues
ERP, order management, transportation, and shipment source systems
Denied-party screening and export compliance tools
General ledger, reconciliation, and payment-impact tracking systems
Fraud, AML, and sanctions monitoring systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on export-operations workflow ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe export progression.
Prepare execution status updates, exception notes, trade execution summaries, and completion evidence records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory export documents, AES statuses, or tenant policies materially change the outcome.
Final ECCN classification, export-license interpretation, or legal advice is required.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, end-use, or consumer-impact conditions change whether the work may safely proceed.
Disclosure, payment, or reconciliation conflicts affect commercial value or downstream customer impact.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, regulated advice, filing authority, or completion evidence.
Requests that require legal, licensed, policy-override, or shipment-approval authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while ITN, exemption citation, departure evidence, or document-release evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Filing or advancing an export without the minimum document set or written filing authority.
Treating unresolved AES fatal errors or contradictory verify messages as completed work.
Allowing ambiguous Schedule B, ECCN, destination, or licensing-reference evidence to silently drive progression instead of escalation.
Ignoring TBML, sanctions, or export-evasion indicators embedded in invoice, party, or destination data.
Closing a case without traceable evidence of EEI disposition, ITN or exemption citation, carrier-document release, departure, or approved closure.
Applying public export defaults when tenant policy requires stricter screening, approvals, or record-retention controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definition of acceptable departure evidence when carrier milestones and internal shipment systems lag each other
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant precedence rules and block completion until the designated system-of-record state is confirmed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether tenant policy requires export operations specialists to stop before any post-departure export-report correction or reconciliation step
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Preserve the public baseline and route to compliance or finance owners when tenant policy extends or narrows the lane.
